Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search emails for Greg Pallone or Swift Relocations or "Denver" using search_emails (query: "Greg Pallone" or "Swift" or "Denver crew" or similar). Find Greg's trigger email (email_greg_chloe_denver_priority_001) from greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com to chloe.vance@moveops.com, subject "April 28 Denver crew priority needed," describing two conflicting Denver pickups on April 28: (1) a San Diego to Boston move with Denver connection pickup, and (2) a Tampa to Denver direct move. Greg asks which one to prioritize and suggests Heartland as an alternative vendor.
OE 2 - Search Airtable relocation records for moves involving Denver as origin or destination using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (field: "Destination", value: "Denver" or similar). Find Deshawn Hartley's record (recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver): Mosaic Robotics, Tampa FL to Denver CO, Status: In Progress, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel, with a hard April 19 delivery deadline for the Automate 2026 trade show. Discovery: Suki Patel coordinates both this move and the BrightLoop move (OE 3), which is how the same person ended up booking Swift's Denver crew for two pickups on the same day.
OE 3 - Search Airtable for the San Diego to Boston move using airtable_search_records (field: "Origin", value: "San Diego" or field: "Name", value: "Jordan Ekwueme" or similar). Find Jordan Ekwueme's record (recReloc00000008): BrightLoop Analytics, San Diego CA to Boston MA, Assigned Coordinator: Suki Patel. This is the San Diego to Boston move from Greg's email. The Denver connection pickup is not visible in the Airtable record itself; the agent must find it via Linear.
OE 4 - Search Linear for issues related to Jordan Ekwueme or BrightLoop or Denver pickup using linear_list_issues (query: "Jordan Ekwueme" or "Denver connection" or "BrightLoop" or similar). Find the Linear issue issue_brightloop_ekwueme_denver_pickup, title: "Coordinate Jordan Ekwueme Denver connection pickup," assigned to Suki Patel (moveops_suki_patel), state: to_do, due April 28. Description confirms Suki is coordinating the Denver connection pickup and states: "Blessing asked me to cover the BrightLoop Boston move while she is still stuck on Marcus Webb fallout." Discovery: the Denver connection pickup on April 28 is the second item conflicting with Deshawn Hartley's pickup on the same date — both booked by Suki through Swift's Denver crew.
OE 5 - Search Slack for Blessing's handoff message using conversations_search_messages (query: "Blessing" and "BrightLoop" or "Suki" and "Boston" or similar) or browse conversations_history on channel C006 (#operations). Find Blessing Okafor's message (channel C006, April 24 6:42 PM UTC, ts: 1777056120.000000): "Quick heads up: I asked Suki to cover the Boston move for BrightLoop while I'm still tied up with Marcus Webb's vehicle delay." Discovery: Blessing informally handed off the BrightLoop move to Suki via an unthreaded, untagged Slack message in #operations without notifying Chloe directly. This is the root cause of the double-booking: Suki inherited the BrightLoop move from Blessing, was already coordinating Deshawn Hartley's Tampa-to-Denver move, and booked Swift's Denver crew for both pickups on April 28.
OE 6 - Look up Greg Pallone's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone"). Find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, Vendor Account Manager at Swift Relocations, Denver.
OE 7 - Look up Heartland Movers contact using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or similar). Find Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) or Lisa Kwan (lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com) as the alternative vendor contacts.
OE 8 - Send an email (via reply_to_email or send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com telling Greg which move to prioritize with Swift's Denver crew. The email should specify which of the two pickups Swift should handle on April 28 and confirm MoveOps will shift the other to an alternative vendor. Write action.
OE 9 - Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to Heartland Movers (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com or lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com) or another alternative vendor to line up coverage for the move that Swift cannot cover on April 28. The email should include the pickup details (origin, destination, date, employee name). Write action.
OE 10 - Send an email or Slack message to Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com) and/or Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com) notifying them about the changes: that the Swift double-booking is being resolved, which vendor is now covering which move, and that informal handoffs without notifying Chloe caused this issue. Write action.
OE 11 - Create a Linear issue (via linear_create_issue) to track the undocumented handoff pattern. Title should reference Blessing's informal coordinator handoff or record-keeping gap (e.g., "Track undocumented coordinator handoff - BrightLoop / Blessing Okafor" or similar). Description should document what happened: Blessing handed off the BrightLoop move to Suki via an unthreaded Slack message without notifying Chloe, which led to the double-booking because Suki was already managing Deshawn Hartley's Denver move. Write action.
OE 12 - Post a message in Slack #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message with a full breakdown of the situation: which two moves were double-booked on April 28, who booked them (Suki, inheriting from Blessing's informal handoff), how the double-booking happened, which move is staying with Swift and which is being shifted to an alternative vendor, and the accountability issue (Blessing's undocumented handoff). Write action.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Greg asks which one to prioritize and suggests Heartland as an alternative vendor.

Discovery: Blessing informally handed off the BrightLoop move to Suki via an unthreaded, untagged Slack message in #operations without notifying Chloe directly. This is the root cause of the double-booking: Suki inherited the BrightLoop move from Blessing, was already coordinating Deshawn Hartley's Tampa-to-Denver move, and booked Swift's Denver crew for both pickups on April 28.
OE 6 - Look up Greg Pallone's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Greg Pallone"). Find greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, Vendor Account Manager at Swift Relocations, Denver.
OE 7 - Look up Heartland Movers contact using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Heartland" or "Jake Loomis" or similar). Find Jake Loomis (jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) or Lisa Kwan (lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com) as the alternative vendor contacts.

OE 10 - Send an email or Slack message to Blessing Okafor (blessing.okafor@moveops.com) and/or Suki Patel (suki.patel@moveops.com) notifying them about the changes: that the Swift double-booking is being resolved, which vendor is now covering which move, and that informal handoffs without notifying Chloe caused this issue. Title should reference Blessing's informal coordinator handoff or record-keeping gap (e.g., "Track undocumented coordinator handoff - BrightLoop / Blessing Okafor" or similar). OE 12 - Post a message in Slack #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message with a full breakdown of the situation: which two moves were double-booked on April 28, who booked them (Suki, inheriting from Blessing's informal handoff), how the double-booking happened, which move is staying with Swift and which is being shifted to an alternative vendor, and the accountability issue (Blessing's undocumented handoff). 1 An email was sent (via send_email or reply_to_email) from [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com).

✓Run #6The agent used reply_to_email with sender chloe.vance@moveops.com and email_id 'email_greg_chloe_denver_priority_001', which is Greg Pallone's email. The reply is addressed 'Hi Greg' and signed by Chloe Vance.
2 The email to Greg Pallone specifies which of the two April 28 Denver pickups Swift should handle and confirms MoveOps will arrange an alternative vendor for the other pickup.
outcome

✓Run #2The email body clearly states 'PRIORITIZE: The San Diego to Boston move (Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop Analytics)' and 'RELEASE: The Tampa to Denver supplemental pickup (Deshawn Hartley / Mosaic Robotics)', with a clear statement that MoveOps is reassigning the Hartley pickup to an alternate vendor.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly says 'Please keep your Denver crew on the San Diego to Boston move (Jordan Ekwueme / BrightLoop Analytics)' as the priority, and 'We'll pull the Deshawn Hartley supplemental pickup... off your board. I'm reaching out to Heartland Movers to cover the Denver receiving on that one.' This clearly specifies the priority and confirms an alternative vendor will handle the other.
✓Run #4The email body clearly states 'Keep Swift's Denver crew on the Jordan Ekwueme move' and 'We'll pull the Deshawn Hartley supplemental pickup off your board. I'm reaching out to Heartland now to cover it.' Both a clear priority decision and the alternative vendor plan are stated.
✓Run #5The email to Greg explicitly states 'PRIORITY FOR SWIFT DENVER CREW ON APRIL 28: → Jordan Ekwueme' and 'MOVE OFF SWIFT BOARD: → Deshawn Hartley', and confirms MoveOps is reaching out to Lakeline Logistics to cover the Deshawn supplemental pickup.
✓Run #6The email to Greg explicitly says 'Please prioritize the Jordan Ekwueme move (San Diego to Boston, Denver connection pickup)' and 'I'm reaching out to Heartland Movers this morning to cover the Hartley supplemental, so you can release that from your Denver crew's Tuesday routing.' Both a clear priority decision and alternative vendor confirmation are present.
3 An email was sent (via send_email) from [chloe.vance@moveops.com](mailto:chloe.vance@moveops.com) to a Heartland Movers contact ([jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com](mailto:jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com) or [lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com](mailto:lisa.kwan@heartlandmovers.com)) to cover the move Swift cannot handle on April 28.

4 The email to the alternative vendor includes the pickup date (April 28), Denver as the pickup location, and the relevant move details (employee name, origin, and destination).
outcome
 ✓✗✓✓✗✓ 67%

✗Run #2Because the alternative vendor email was sent to Lakeline Logistics (not Heartland Movers as required), this criterion fails. While the Lakeline email does contain April 28, Denver, and Deshawn Hartley's details, the criterion specifically evaluates the email to the Heartland Movers contact.

✓Run #6The email to Lisa Kwan at Heartland explicitly mentions 'Tuesday, April 28', 'Tampa, FL' as pickup location with 'Denver, CO' as destination, 'Deshawn Hartley' as the employee, and 'Mosaic Robotics' as the company.
5 The Jordan Ekwueme Airtable relocation record (recReloc00000008) was updated (via airtable_update_records) to correct the Status field from 'Completed' to a non-completed status (e.g., 'In Progress' or similar), since the Denver connection pickup on April 28 is still pending in Linear.
outcome
 ✗✗✓✓✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent updated recReloc00000008 (Jordan Ekwueme) but only changed the 'Special Requirements' field. It did NOT update the 'Status' field from 'Completed' to 'In Progress' or any non-completed status, even though the Airtable record showed Status='Completed' while the Linear issue (issue_brightloop_ekwueme_denver_pickup) was still in 'to_do' state due April 28.
✗Run #2The agent's airtable_update_records calls targeted recKeikoTanakaColumbusSeattle, recDeshawnHartleyTampaDenver, and recRajPatelAuditNote0411. The Jordan Ekwueme record (recReloc00000008) was never updated; its Status field was not corrected from 'Completed' to a non-completed status to match the pending Linear issue.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_update_records with record id 'recReloc00000008' (Jordan Ekwueme's record) and set Status to 'In Progress', correcting the previous 'Completed' status to reflect the pending Denver connection pickup.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_update_records targeting record id 'recReloc00000008' (Jordan Ekwueme) and set 'Status': 'In Progress', correcting the incorrect 'Completed' status.
